name: Dawn
first: First mission to orbit two extraterrestrial bodies — Vesta, then Ceres
description: Dawn proved ion propulsion at planetary scale: it matched orbits with massive Vesta, left, and spiralled down onto dwarf planet Ceres — the largest object in the asteroid belt. Bright spots in Occator crater became one of the mission's iconic mysteries, later tied to briny eruptions from a deep reservoir. This catalogue entry uses Ceres as the primary destination; Vesta is covered in the narrative above.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Delta II lifts Dawn toward Vesta and Ceres on a low-thrust spiral highway.

events.label: CERES CAPTURE
events.note: Ion engines brake Dawn into orbit around Ceres; mapping campaign begins.
